commands.repair_reason: - [P2] No narrow automated repair remains; maintainers need to finish normal PR review, CI validation, and the linked bug landing decision.; Cleared: No new security or supply-chain concern was found; the diff is limited to TTS runtime cleanup and tests and reduces hidden reasoning/control-token leakage into spoken output. (sha=4be97aa0e954f7287e21d16d297d38e44e6a0bdf)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds TTS summary-output sanitization in `src/tts/tts-core.ts` and focused core/contract tests for prompt echoes, hidden scaffolding, length capping, and false-positive preservation.

PR surface: Source +147, Tests +311. Total +458 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible with high confidence: current main returns raw joined summary model text and the auto-TTS caller assigns that returned summary directly to `textForAudio`. A controlled `completeSimple` response containing prompt echoes and reasoning scaffolding exercises the leak path without needing live provider credentials.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] No narrow automated repair remains; maintainers need to finish normal PR review, CI validation, and the linked bug landing decision.

target.title: fix(tts): sanitize summarized speech text
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #90364.

TTS summarization now cleans summary-model output before speech synthesis:

- reuses the shared assistant-visible text sanitizer
- removes exact echoed source prompt blocks, echoed summary prompt instructions, and complete or EOF-truncated standalone echoed `<text_to_summarize>` prompt blocks
- strips only prompt-specific leading summarizer preambles while preserving valid first-person and user-request summary prose
- preserves usable summary content after colon/dash prompt preambles without dropping subject-bearing user-summary clauses
- preserves valid inline mentions of prompt tags in summaries
- caps the sanitized summary at the requested speech target length

## Root Cause

`src/tts/tts-core.ts` joined `TextContent` blocks from `completeSimple` and returned that raw string as `summary.summary`. Auto-TTS then assigned that value directly to `textForAudio`, so reasoning tags, model control tokens, prompt echo, or echoed original text could be spoken.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Long-response TTS summaries no longer pass hidden reasoning/control scaffolding, echoed summarization prompt instructions, echoed summarization prompt text, exact echoed source prompt blocks, complete or truncated standalone echoed `<text_to_summarize>` content, or control tokens through to the audio text candidate. The review feedback cases are also covered: legitimate first-person summary prose is preserved, valid first-person summaries that mention `key points` are preserved, valid summaries starting with `The user asked me to summarize ...` are preserved before periods and separators, bare `The user asked me to summarize.` prompt echoes are stripped, usable content after bare colon-style prompt preambles is preserved, source text containing literal prompt close tags no longer defeats exact prompt-block cleanup, summaries that mention `<text_to_summarize>` inline are preserved, and echoed prompt blocks returned in a later `TextContent` block are stripped after text-block joining.

Real environment tested: Dedicated local macOS OpenClaw source checkout on branch `codex/fix-tts-summary-sanitization` at commit `4be97aa0e9`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm exec tsx /private/tmp/openclaw-tts-summary-proof.ts`

Evidence after fix: Terminal capture from the local OpenClaw source run:

```console
$ pnpm exec tsx /private/tmp/openclaw-tts-summary-proof.ts
OpenClaw TTS summary proof
sanitizedSummary="Concise audible summary."
sanitizedOutputLength=24
targetLength=120
leakedForbiddenTokens=none
firstPersonSummary="I need to keep the key points from today's review. The release moves to Friday."
firstPersonPreserved=yes
inlineTagSummary="The docs mention `<text_to_summarize>` as a literal prompt marker. The release moves to Friday."
inlineTagPreserved=yes
joinedPromptSummary="Concise audible summary."
bareUserPromptSummary="Deployment was delayed until Friday."
audioText=Concise audible summary.
```

Observed result after fix: The sanitized audio text candidate was `Concise audible summary.`. The proof input included summarizer preamble, `<think>` reasoning, `<|assistant|>` control token, tool-call XML, and an echoed standalone `<text_to_summarize>` block; the produced audio text candidate had `leakedForbiddenTokens=none` and stayed under the requested target length. The same production `summarizeText` cleanup path also preserved `I need to keep the key points from today's review. The release moves to Friday.` with `firstPersonPreserved=yes`, preserved the inline tag summary with `inlineTagPreserved=yes`, stripped a joined later-block prompt echo to `joinedPromptSummary="Concise audible summary."`, and stripped a bare user-summary sentence echo to `bareUserPromptSummary="Deployment was delayed until Friday."`.

What was not tested: Live Microsoft/MiniMax audio playback was not run because provider credentials and audio playback setup were not available in this local checkout.

Proof limitations or environment constraints: This proof exercises the production `summarizeText` cleanup path from the PR worktree with controlled provider text. It verifies the text handed toward speech synthesis, not the downstream Microsoft audio renderer.

## CI / Review State

- Review comments from ClawSweeper and Codex on earlier heads were addressed through `4be97aa0e9` by tightening prompt-echo detection, preserving text-block boundaries before cleanup, stripping echoed prompt instructions, preserving first-person summary prose, preserving valid first-person `key points` summaries, preserving valid inline prompt-tag mentions, preserving valid `The user asked me to summarize ...` summary prose before periods and separators, stripping bare period-form user-summary prompt echoes, preserving content after bare colon/dash prompt preambles, stripping EOF-truncated standalone `<text_to_summarize>` echoes, and removing exact echoed source prompt blocks even when the source contains literal closing tags.
- GitHub workflows are running on the current PR head; current live status is available in the PR Checks tab. Local focused verification below passed on `4be97aa0e9`.
- Codex autoreview was attempted locally after commit `18435b1380`, but the bundled helper's Codex CLI rejected the local config value `service_tier=priority`; this PR relies on focused local proof plus GitHub CI/review.

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` in the dedicated worktree to hydrate dependencies
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/tts/tts-core.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/tts.contract.test.ts packages/speech-core/src/tts.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-utils.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers.sanitizeuserfacingtext.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec tsx /private/tmp/openclaw-tts-summary-proof.ts`

Focused verification passed across 3 Vitest shards: 2 unit-fast files / 165 tests, 2 unit files / 54 tests, and 1 plugin-contract file / 42 tests.
target.author: monkeyleet
